mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.12% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors frontrun earnings downgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 5.4% near elevated levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% and HOUST declines > 5% over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 436772M and HOUST at 926K units
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 105.5 indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 105.1786 near recent highs
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth does not precede positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 236 combined with elevated federal funds rate above 0.5% signals monetary policy tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when both inflation and rates are elevated, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth above 141500K indicates strong labor market momentum and consumer spending capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
regime_hint: expansion phase with labor market strength
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances when PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.15% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) declining below 16300B signals economic deceleration and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances following GDP declines, or the correlation between GDPC1 changes and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals stagflationary pressure and supply-side deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as margin compression fears dominate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises, or the joint interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression model.
